analysisSets.name: Intent-to-Treat Population, Observed Cases Without Imputation (ADSL.ITTFL='Y')
analysisSets.label: Intent-to-Treat Population, Observed Cases Without Imputation (ADSL.ITTFL='Y')

codeTemplate.code:
denom_n <- length(unique(df2_analysisidhere$USUBJID))
df3_analysisidhere <- df2_analysisidhere |>
  dplyr::group_by(anavarhere) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    OP_N     = dplyr::n_distinct(USUBJID),
    OP_DENOM = denom_n,
    .groups  = 'drop'
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(OP_PCT = 100 * OP_N / OP_DENOM) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    dplyr::starts_with('OP_'),
    names_to  = 'operation',
    values_to = 'res'
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(pattern = 'XXX')

df3_analysisidhere <- df2_analysisidhere |>
  dplyr::distinct(USUBJID) |>
  dplyr::summarise(res = dplyr::n()) |>
  dplyr::mutate(operation = 'OP_N', pattern = 'XXX')

methods.name: Summary Statistics - Continuous
methods.label: Summary Statistics - Continuous
methods.description: n, mean, SD, median, Q1, Q3, min, max

codeTemplate.code:
df3_analysisidhere <- df2_analysisidhere |>
  dplyr::select(USUBJID, anavarhere) |>
  unique() |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    OP_N      = sum(!is.na(anavarhere)),
    OP_MEAN   = mean(anavarhere, na.rm = TRUE),
    OP_SD     = stats::sd(anavarhere, na.rm = TRUE),
    OP_MEDIAN = stats::median(anavarhere, na.rm = TRUE),
    OP_Q1     = stats::quantile(anavarhere, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    OP_Q3     = stats::quantile(anavarhere, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    OP_MIN    = suppressWarnings(min(anavarhere, na.rm = TRUE)),
    OP_MAX    = suppressWarnings(max(anavarhere, na.rm = TRUE))
  ) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(dplyr::everything(), names_to = 'operation', values_to = 'res') |>
  dplyr::mutate(pattern = 'XXX.X')
## Clopper-Pearson exact confidence interval -- computed by the arsbridge engine (cardx / base R).
## Cochran-Mantel-Haenszel test -- computed by the arsbridge engine (cardx / base R).

methods.name: Unsupported analysis (manual)
methods.label: Unsupported analysis (manual)
methods.description: requires Cochran-Mantel-Haenszel test; exact confidence interval (Clopper-Pearson); Newcombe / Wilson difference interval; p-value (hypothesis test); multiple imputation / NRI -- beyond arsbridge's descriptive {cards} methods

codeTemplate.code:
## Manual derivation required -- requires Cochran-Mantel-Haenszel test; exact confidence interval (Clopper-Pearson); Newcombe / Wilson difference interval; p-value (hypothesis test); multiple imputation / NRI -- beyond arsbridge's descriptive {cards} methods
## arsbridge reserves a manual_pending ARD cell for this analysis;
## compute it with a validated script and fill the reserved row
## (see ars_manual_worklist()).

denom_n <- length(unique(df2_analysisidhere$USUBJID))
df3_analysisidhere <- df2_analysisidhere |>
  dplyr::distinct(USUBJID, anavarhere) |>
  dplyr::group_by(anavarhere) |>
  dplyr::summarise(OP_N = dplyr::n(), .groups = 'drop') |>
  dplyr::mutate(OP_PCT = 100 * OP_N / denom_n) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    dplyr::starts_with('OP_'),
    names_to  = 'operation',
    values_to = 'res'
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(pattern = 'XXX')

## Listing: pass through the filtered ADaM rows as-is.
df3_analysisidhere <- df2_analysisidhere |>
  dplyr::mutate(operation = 'OP_PASS', res = NA_real_, pattern = 'X')

subSections.text: Percentages are based on the number of randomized subjects in each treatment group. The placebo arm includes subjects through Week 16 only; from Week 16 onward, placebo subjects were re-randomized 1:1 to UPADALIMIB 15 mg or 30 mg.

outputs.name: T-14-1-2
outputs.label: Subject Demographics and Baseline Atopic Dermatitis Characteristics

displays.displayTitle: Subject Demographics and Baseline Atopic Dermatitis Characteristics

subSections.text: EASI ranges from 0-72 (higher = worse). vIGA-AD ranges from 0 (clear) to 4 (severe). WP-NRS ranges from 0 (no itch) to 10 (worst itch imaginable). POEM ranges from 0-28 (higher = worse). DLQI ranges from 0-30 (CDLQI used for adolescents).

shell_layout.label: Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander
Prior Systemic Therapy for AD, n (%)

outputs.name: T-14-1-3
outputs.label: Concomitant Medications by ATC Level 3 Class and Preferred Term

displays.displayTitle: Concomitant Medications by ATC Level 3 Class and Preferred Term

subSections.text: Concomitant medications are coded using WHO Drug Dictionary. A medication is considered concomitant if taken at any time between first dose of study drug and 30 days after last dose. Subjects are counted once per Preferred Term, regardless of frequency of use.

outputs.name: T-14-1-4
outputs.label: Topical Corticosteroid (TCS) and Topical Calcineurin Inhibitor (TCI) Use Through Week 140

displays.displayTitle: Topical Corticosteroid (TCS) and Topical Calcineurin Inhibitor (TCI) Use Through Week 140

subSections.text: Topical therapies for AD include TCS and TCI. Use is captured at each scheduled study visit for the prior 4-week period. The denominator at each visit is the number of subjects on study at that visit.

shell_layout.label: Subjects with ≥ 1 TCS or TCI use during study
Did not receive any topical therapy

subSections.text: Duration of exposure = (date of last dose - date of first dose + 1) / 365.25. Cumulative dose = sum of all daily doses received. Dose intensity = (cumulative dose / planned dose during exposure period) × 100.

outputs.name: T-14-2-1
outputs.label: Proportion of Subjects Achieving EASI 75 at Week 16 (Primary Endpoint)

displays.displayTitle: Proportion of Subjects Achieving EASI 75 at Week 16 (Primary Endpoint)

subSections.text: [a] Number evaluable = subjects with non-missing EASI assessment at Week 16.
[b] 95% CIs computed using the Clopper-Pearson exact method.
[c] Difference in response rate computed using the Newcombe method.
[d] Cochran-Mantel-Haenszel test stratified by baseline vIGA-AD severity (3 vs. 4), age group (Adolescent vs. Adult), and geographic region.
[e] Subjects who received rescue therapy or who had missing EASI at Week 16 are imputed as non-responders.

outputs.name: T-14-2-2
outputs.label: Proportion of Subjects Achieving EASI Response Endpoints Through Week 140

displays.displayTitle: Proportion of Subjects Achieving EASI Response Endpoints Through Week 140

subSections.text: N' = number of subjects with non-missing EASI at the visit (denominator). 95% CIs computed using normal approximation to the binomial.
Observed cases analysis: subjects with missing EASI at the visit are excluded from both numerator and denominator.

outputs.name: T-14-3-1
outputs.label: Overview of Treatment-Emergent Adverse Events Through Week 140 (Exposure-Adjusted)

displays.displayTitle: Overview of Treatment-Emergent Adverse Events Through Week 140 (Exposure-Adjusted)

columns.label: UPADALIMIB 15 mg (N=200) PY = x,xxx.x E (E/100 PY)
UPADALIMIB 30 mg (N=200) PY = x,xxx.x E (E/100 PY)

subSections.text: E = number of events. E/100 PY = (number of events / total patient-years of exposure) × 100. TEAE = adverse event with onset on or after first dose of study drug, or pre-existing event that worsens after first dose. Subjects with multiple events of the same type are counted multiple times in E.

shell_layout.label: TEAE possibly related to study drug
TEAE leading to study drug discontinuation

outputs.name: T-14-3-2
outputs.label: Most Common Treatment-Emergent Adverse Events Through Week 140 (≥ 5 E/100 PY in any treatment group, by Preferred Term)

displays.displayTitle: Most Common Treatment-Emergent Adverse Events Through Week 140 (≥ 5 E/100 PY in any treatment group, by Preferred Term)

subSections.text: Most common TEAEs are defined as Preferred Terms with exposure-adjusted event rate ≥ 5 E/100 PY in either UPADALIMIB treatment group through Week 140. Adverse events are coded using MedDRA (current version at database lock). PTs are sorted in descending order by combined event rate across UPADALIMIB arms.

outputs.name: F-14-2-1
outputs.label: EASI 75 / 90 / 100 Response Rates Over Time Through Week 140

displays.displayTitle: EASI 75 / 90 / 100 Response Rates Over Time Through Week 140

subSections.text: [FIGURE PLACEHOLDER — line plot generated by SAS PROC SGPLOT]
Y-axis: Proportion of subjects (%) (with 95% CI error bars)
Series: one line per treatment arm (UPADALIMIB 15 mg, UPADALIMIB 30 mg, Placebo)

source_datasets: ADEFF (PARAMCD='EASI75|EASI90|EASI100'). EACH TIMEPOINT PLOTS THE OBSERVED-CASE RESPONSE RATE WITH A 95% NORMAL-APPROXIMATION CONFIDENCE INTERVAL

subSections.text: TEAEs are sorted by USUBJID, then by AE start date. AESI flag and adjudication status (where applicable) are shown in Listing 16.2.4.5.
Vital signs measured prior to dose administration after subject has been seated and at rest for at least 5 minutes. Height collected at screening only.
